executiveSummary.text: Nvidia surpassed $5T market cap Friday (+4.32%, closed at $208.27), AI infrastructure trade resuming with sector-wide chip gains. Cramer endorsed 'Own It, Don't Trade It' for semis.
Fed holding at 3.50-3.75% — DOJ dropped Powell probe, clearing path for Kevin Warsh (nominee). Warsh signaling extended hold, more hawkish than Powell. Rate cap environment persists.

Oil pulled back sharply Friday: WTI -11.45% to ~$83.85/bbl. Iran foreign minister said Hormuz passage remains open during ceasefire — eased inflation fears, boosted risk appetite, pressured gold lower.

Gold extended gains Thursday but slipped Friday as oil fell + dollar firmed. Real rate dynamic: lower oil → lower inflation → higher real yields = headwind for gold near-term despite geopolitical backdrop.
Singapore positioning as neutral AI ground for Sino-US rivalry; also planning Asia gold trading hub. Singapore REITs seeing retail rent pressures (Orchard submarket negative absorption).

metrics.label: MU (Micron)
metrics.change: All-time high hit earlier in April

usGlobal.what: Nvidia cleared $5T market cap Friday on AI optimism
usGlobal.why: AI infrastructure demand insatiable; hyperscaler capex commitments; Intel earnings spark sector-wide re-rating
usGlobal.implication: AI Infra sleeve (420k/16%) benefits directly. Semis + equipment core holding thesis intact. MU at ATH validates memory/HBM scarcity theme.

usGlobal.what: Fed holds at 3.50-3.75%; Warsh likely next chair
usGlobal.why: DOJ dropped Powell probe. Warsh academic work suggests concern about money supply, more consistent with extended hold. Rate-sensitive assets partially reprieved but curve steepening risk remains.
usGlobal.implication: SG Banks benefit marginally from stable rate environment. SGD fixed income holders insulated. WI 3M SIBOR tied to Fed path — no immediate change.

usGlobal.why: Intel reported stronger-than-expected Q1; Lip-Bu Tan (new CEO) excited about Tesla partnership on 14A foundry chips. AMD +12%, ASML, AMAT, KLAC also higher.
usGlobal.implication: Equipment/semiconductor ecosystem momentum intact. Broad-based AI infrastructure buildout thesis reinforced across equipment names.

usGlobal.what: Mali armed attacks, Falklands US 'review', Georgia wildfires
usGlobal.why: Geopolitical risk vectors multiplying. Mali attacks (2hrs ago) = West Africa supply chain/risk premium. Falklands = UK-LATAM diplomatic risk. Georgia = climate/damage costs.
usGlobal.implication: Mali crisis could pressure uranium supply (Niger/HPTEC). West Africa instability not yet in prices. Gold response muted Friday (real rate dynamic dominant).

singapore.what: Singapore planning Asia gold trading hub
singapore.why: MAS + industry initiative to strengthen trading, clearing, storage infrastructure. Part of Singapore's financial centre diversification.
singapore.implication: SG banks with precious metals capabilities benefit. DBS has strong commodities desk. Long-term thesis: Singapore as Asia's safe haven for physical + financial gold aligns with portfolio's metals position.

singapore.what: Singapore emerging as neutral AI ground
singapore.why: Chinese AI startups seeking operation beyond government reach; US firms seeking foreign talent without strict visa rules. Singapore positioned as data/compute bridge.
singapore.implication: Singapore's geopolitical neutrality thesis strengthening. Middle East/Hormuz tensions + Sino-US tech war = more flows through Singapore. Data centre REITs + infrastructure names benefit.

singapore.what: Singapore retail rents fell; Orchard submarket negative absorption
singapore.why: Orchard negative net absorption -4,000 sqm (extending prior quarter decline). Overall retail market rents softer. Consumer spending caution.
singapore.implication: Retail REITs face headwinds. CICT (Centrally managed, more resilient) vs FCT (more exposed to suburban). Portfolio's REITs at 7.7% — monitor occupancy and rental reversions.

commodities.what: Oil pulled back sharply Friday: WTI -11.45% to $83.85
commodities.why: Iran ceasefire + Hormuz stays open (per Iranian FM). Pentagon reported 6-month mine clearance timeline. Earlier spike to $106+ on Hormuz closure fears unwinding.
commodities.implication: Oil shock de-escalation = inflation headwind eases = real rates potentially rise faster than expected. 2nd order: Energy equities face pressure, but consumer spending/purchasing power recovers. Portfolio has no direct oil exposure (metals thesis).

commodities.what: Gold extended gains Thursday on weaker dollar + Iran news
commodities.why: Weaker USD supports gold. Iran ceasefire signal reduced safe-haven premium, but some haven bid persisted Thursday. Friday gold slipped as oil fell + dollar firmed.
commodities.implication: Gold is a REAL RATE hedge, NOT a crisis hedge. Friday's move confirms: when geopolitical tension eases → oil down → real rates up → gold headwind. Portfolio's 175k metals sleeve partially exposed. Key level: $4,600-4,700 range.

commodities.why: Industrial + monetary metal. Copper also under pressure from dollar strength + growth concerns. Gold/silver ratio still elevated historically.
commodities.implication: Silver more volatile than gold. 175k metals position includes silver — industrial demand from solar/AI/semiconductors is long-term tailwind, but near-term momentum muted.

narratives.title: AI Infrastructure Buildout — Unstoppable Until Proven Otherwise
narratives.description: Nvidia $5T, Micron ATH, Intel surging. Jim Cramer saying 'own it, don't trade it' reflects institutional consensus that AI capex supercycle has years to run. Semiconductors from silicon wafers to equipment to packaging all participating.

narratives.title: Fed Regime Change — Powell Out, Warsh In
narratives.description: DOJ probe dropped. Kevin Warsh (academic, former Fed governor, anti-inflation stance) likely to replace Powell. Warsh's own writings suggest concern about money supply and inflation psychology. Market repricing terminal rate higher if Warsh confirms 'extended hold' stance.
narratives.source: CNBC, BBC, Economic Times, Wells Fargo

narratives.title: Oil Geopolitical Risk Premium Expanding and Contracting
narratives.description: Iran ceasefire + mine clearance timeline + Hormuz remaining open = rapid de-escalation of oil shock. WTI -11.45% in one session. Brent went from $106+ (peak fear) to ~$96. This is the most volatile oil move of the year. Supply shock now priced out; demand destruction not yet visible.
narratives.priceImpact: positive for risk assets, negative for energy equities

narratives.title: Gold — Real Rate Hedge vs. Crisis Hedge Confusion
narratives.description: Gold had been rallying as Iran tensions escalated, but Friday's move (gold slipped as oil fell) exposes the real rate mechanism. Lower oil → lower inflation → higher real yields → gold headwind. The lazy 'geopolitical risk = gold up' narrative is being tested. Real rate regime matters more than headlines.

secondOrderEffects.text: Oil collapse from $106+ to $83.85 = real yield tailwind for equities. Consumer purchasing power recovers, inflation expectations moderate, Fed gets room to breathe. But Warsh as Fed chair could mean 'higher for longer' even with falling energy prices — offsetting some equity benefit.
secondOrderEffects.implication: Equity bull case intact but rate-sensitive SG banks may face margin pressure if curve flattens. HY credit spreads may tighten as energy-sector stress recedes.

secondOrderEffects.text: Nvidia $5T and semiconductor equipment makers hitting records = crowding risk. When everything that can go right does, any micro crack (export controls tightening, China slowdown worse than expected, AMD/Azure custom chip competition) could cause sharp de-rating. Momentum is a factor until it isn't.
secondOrderEffects.implication: AI Infra sleeve 420k/16% — consider trailing stops or rebalancing discipline. NVDA at $208 is not cheap at ~35-40x forward earnings, but AI growth can justify for 1-2 years.

secondOrderEffects.text: Mali attacks (armed groups, simultaneous multi-site) = Sahel instability. Mali is a uranium producer. HPTEC (Denison Mines/Yellow Cake) and global uranium supply chains could face disruption. Niger+Mail = ~15-20% of global uranium production. This is a 3rd order effect not yet priced.
secondOrderEffects.implication: Uranium position (GRID/URNM) — geopolitical tail risk here. West Africa + Central Asia = two simultaneous risk zones for uranium. Monitor spot uranium price closely.

secondOrderEffects.text: Singapore gold hub = signal that Singapore wants to capture physical gold flows from Asian buyers (China, India, SE Asia). With US dollar weaponized (Russia sanctions), gold as reserve asset gaining institutional acceptance. MAS initiative could bring significant AUM to Singapore.
secondOrderEffects.implication: SG precious metals ecosystem stocks (DBS commodities desk) long-term beneficiaries. Gold hoarding by Asian central banks = structural demand support for 5-10 years.

investment.thesis: AI Infrastructure: Semis, Equipment, Energy — own what AI depends on. Nvidia + equipment names remain core. MU at ATH validates memory scarcity. AMD catching up to NVDA in inference chip. ASML: monopoly on EUV, benefits from every chipmaker capex.
investment.trigger: Q1 earnings revisions upward; TSMC capex guidance increase; AI model scaling continuing.
investment.whyNow: NVDA $5T is a signal — when leader hits all-time high, ecosystem still has legs. Semis at record relative performance vs S&P500.
investment.invalidator: Export controls tighten further; AI capex slows; custom ASICs (Google TPUs, Amazon Trainium, Meta chips) displace GPU demand faster than expected.

investment.thesis: Singapore REITs: Wait for better entry. Retail headwinds + interest rate sensitivity + office oversupply. Ascendas (A15U) and CICT (CY6U) more resilient (industrial/data centre exposure). FCT (dual-class suburban retail) faces consumer spending caution. Wait for 2H2026 when rate cuts potentially materialize.
investment.trigger: SIBOR decline; MAS dovish signal; retail sales rebound; REIT dividend yield spread vs SGS widens to 200bp+.
investment.whyNow: Fed on hold but Warsh incoming = more hawkish tone likely. Rate sensitivity for REITs remains elevated. Current yields don't compensate adequately for rate risk.
investment.invalidator: Fed pivots sooner than expected; Singapore economy outperforms; data centre demand surges beyond projections.

trading.thesis: Gold: Short-term headwind from falling oil + stronger dollar = real yield rise. Watch $4,600 support. Real rate mechanism means gold is not a 'crisis hedge' in inflationary shocks. Iran ceasefire = oil down = inflation expectations moderate = gold headwind. But Warsh as Fed chair = could be 'higher real rates for longer' even with economic slowdown = structural gold headwind.
trading.trigger: Gold breaks below $4,600; DXY above 97; 10Y real yield above 2.5%.
trading.whyNow: Friday's gold slip after Iran ceasefire news confirms real rate mechanism dominating safe-haven narrative. Metals sleeve at 175k/7% — consider reducing if gold breaks below $4,600.
trading.invalidator: Ceasefire collapses; oil spikes again; Warsh more dovish than expected; gold breaks above $4,800 with volume.

trading.thesis: Oil: Geopolitical premium rapidly unwinding. $83.85 WTI is fair value if Hormuz stays open and ceasefire holds. Supply/demand: OPEC+ may cut to support. But demand destruction from tariffs still uncertain. $80-85 WTI = consumer/purchasing power positive, equity tailwind. Energy equities under pressure near-term.
trading.trigger: Ceasefire formally confirmed; OPEC+ production decision; China demand data.
trading.whyNow: Portfolio has no direct oil exposure (metals thesis). Oil decline is net positive for risk assets except energy equities. But watch for surprise: ceasefire collapse = oil back to $100+ quickly.
trading.invalidator: Ceasefire breaks; Hormuz mines not cleared within 6 months; Iranian Revolutionary Guard resumes seizures.

watchlist.thesis: Micron (MU) — memory cycle turning. HBM demand from AI = structural tightness. MU hit $260 ATH earlier in April. At current prices, MU is pricing in excellent fundamentals. Watch for 20%+ correction from ATH as opportunity, or breakout above $260 with volume.
watchlist.trigger: MU Q2 earnings beat; HBM allocation updates; AI server demand data.
watchlist.whyNow: MU is largest portfolio exposure to memory. AI inference = more memory per query. LPDDR5X + HBM3e = tight supply through 2026.
watchlist.invalidator: Samsung/Hynix capacity expansion faster than expected; AI capex slowdown hits memory orders; China chip crackdown impacts MU China revenue.

watchlist.thesis: Uranium (GRID/URNM) + West Africa geopolitical risk. Mali attacks = Sahel instability. Niger+Mali = ~15-20% of global uranium. Kazakhstan = largest producer (~40%). Kazakhstan also has political risk. If spot uranium breaks below $85/lb, it would be a buying opportunity given structural supply deficit.
watchlist.trigger: Uranium spot price; Kazakhstan political developments; West Africa escalation; China nuclear buildout data.
watchlist.whyNow: Portfolio holds uranium (GRID). 3rd order risk: if West Africa instability spreads to Kazakhstan (3rd largest) AND Niger/Mali offline simultaneously = uranium supply shock. Low probability but high impact. Monitor, don't act on low-probability tail risk yet.
watchlist.invalidator: Multiple simultaneous supply disruptions; demand exceeds supply; nuclear renaissance accelerates beyond projections.

riskWatch.name: Fed Chair Transition — Warsh More Hawkish
riskWatch.description: Warsh replacing Powell. Warsh's academic stance: anti-inflation, rules-based monetary framework, concerned about money supply growth. This could mean 'higher for longer' even if economic data softens. 2nd order: Long-duration assets (REITs, long-maturity bonds) face repricing risk. Real rates could rise faster than expected if Warsh signals intolerance for inflation above 2%.
riskWatch.mitigation: Portfolio barbell 40.4% foundation (cash+CPF+index+SG div) insulated. Risk (59.6%) mostly in equities that can grow through rate headwind. Monitor 10Y UST yield — if breaks above 5%, re-evaluate REIT allocation.

riskWatch.name: AI Semiconductor Concentration Risk
riskWatch.description: AI Infra sleeve 420k/16% heavily concentrated in single thematic. NVDA $5T, equipment names at records. Crowding risk. If AI growth disappoints (even one quarter), sharp de-rating likely. Export controls on advanced chips to China remain tail risk.
riskWatch.mitigation: Portfolio already diversified across semis (NVDA, AMD, AVGO, TSM, ASML, AMAT, LRCX, KLAC, MU, SNPS). Semis diversity reduces single-name risk. VWRA+QQQ index provides AI infrastructure + diversification.

riskWatch.name: Mali/Sahel + Kazakhstan Uranium Supply Risk
riskWatch.description: Mali attacks (2 hours ago) + Niger/Mali = ~15-20% of global uranium. Kazakhstan (40% of supply) also has political risk. Low probability simultaneous disruption, but high impact for uranium position (GRID). This is a 3rd order tail risk — not yet priced.
riskWatch.mitigation: Portfolio holds diversified uranium exposure via GRID/URNM ETF (not single names). Real supply disruption would push uranium spot well above $100/lb — current price ~$85-90. Position size appropriate for low-prob tail risk.

riskWatch.name: Oil Ceasefire Collapse
riskWatch.description: Iran ceasefire fragile. Pentagon said mine clearance could take 6 months. One incident (Revolutionary Guard seizure, militia attack on US assets) could reignite Hormuz tensions. Oil spiked 6%+ multiple times in April. If ceasefire breaks, WTI could quickly return to $100+. 2nd order: inflation resurges, Fed can't cut, real rates spike, gold collapses.
riskWatch.mitigation: Portfolio has no direct oil exposure (metals thesis). But energy-adjacent names (some in VWRA/QQQ) could see volatility. Gold position partially hedges oil-shock inflation scenario — gold is real rate hedge, not crisis hedge. Real rate mechanism: if oil spike causes inflation + Fed response = gold ultimately negative.

keyEvents.text: Fed policy meeting — holding at 3.50-3.75% (likely Powell's last)
DOJ drops probe into Fed Chair Jerome Powell
Kevin Warsh expected to replace Powell as Fed chair
Nvidia hits $5T market cap (+4.32%, closed at $208.27 Friday)
Intel surges post-Q1 earnings; Lip-Bu Tan announces Tesla 14A foundry partnership
Micron (MU) hits all-time high ~$260 earlier in April
WTI oil -11.45% Friday to $83.85/bbl; Brent peaked $106+ earlier in week
Iran ceasefire holds; Hormuz passage open (Iranian FM confirmation)
Pentagon briefs mine clearance in Gulf may take 6 months
Singapore planning Asia gold trading hub
Singapore emerging as neutral AI ground for Sino-US tech rivalry
Armed groups launch coordinated attacks across Mali (April 25)
Gold ~$4,700/oz extended gains Thursday, slipped Friday

whatMatters: NVDA $5T + AI infrastructure thesis intact — semis + equipment portfolio thesis validated by market
Fed hold confirmed; Warsh as next chair = more hawkish extended hold; rate environment unchanged for now
Oil de-escalation (Hormuz open) = inflation easing = real rate tailwind for risk assets; but Warsh could offset
Gold NOT a crisis hedge — real rate mechanism dominant; falling oil = gold headwind near-term
Mali attacks = uranium tail risk (low probability, high impact if materializes)
Singapore gold hub + AI neutral ground thesis strengthening
whatToDo: Hold AI Infra sleeve — momentum intact; NVDA at $5T signals institutional conviction
Monitor gold below $4,600 — if breaks, consider trimming metals sleeve or setting stops
Watch Warsh Fed confirmation hearings for hawkish signals — could steepen yield curve
MU at ATH — treat as holding, not new entry. Wait for pullback to $220-230 for accumulation
Hold uranium position but set alert for Kazakhstan + West Africa simultaneous disruption scenario
Singapore REITs — patience. Rate cut timing uncertain; yields not compelling enough vs rate risk
whatToIgnore: Daily oil price volatility — ceasefire fragilities create noise; $80-85 fair value range
Mali attacks (immediate) — uranium supply impact not yet material; watch before acting
Falklands US 'review' — UK-sovereignty issue, limited market impact for SG portfolio
NFL Draft, White House Correspondents' Dinner — zero portfolio relevance
Georgia wildfires — tragic but limited direct commodity/equity market impact
